steps.assistant_content_head:
I'll create a task management page with a modal dialog system that meets all the specified requirements.

First, I'll write the HTML file with inline CSS and JavaScript:

tool_calls.name: write_file
tool_calls.content_head:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Task Management App</title>
    <style>

steps.assistant_content_head: Now I'll render the HTML file to a PNG using a headless browser to verify it meets the requirements:

tool_calls.name: bash
tool_calls.command: python3 -c "from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright; p=sync_playwright().start(); b=p.chromium.launch(); pg=b.new_page(viewport={'width':1920,'height':1080}); pg.goto('file://' + __import__('o

steps.assistant_content_head: Now I'll examine the rendered PNG to verify if the page meets all requirements:

I can see the task management page with 8 task cards arranged in a 2-column grid as required. The cards display task titles, status pills (with appropriate colors: blue for "To Do", yellow for "In Progress", green for "Done"), due dates, and "Edit" buttons.

However, I need to verify the modal functionality which isn't visible in this screenshot. I'll need to test the modal by clicking an "Edit" b
